creator: pgollucci@ridecharge.com
text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA1

portmgr@, I'll take this one --

HTTP_PROXY shouldn't be set in your environment unless you want it affect the
way that fetch(1) works. HTTP_PROXY is documented in fetch(3).

[snippet]
HTTP_PROXY
~  URL of the proxy to use for HTTP requests.  The docu-
~  ment part is ignored.  Only HTTP proxies are sup-
~  ported for HTTP requests.  If no port number is spec-
~  ified, the default is 3128.

Also, your value for this variable is incorrect.  A correct example also from
fetch(3) is
~  HTTP_PROXY=http://<user>:<pwd>@proxy.example.com:<port>

The ports in question don't use this variable so passing it on the make lines
is pointless.

You should probably set this variable in your shell init scripts to avoid
having to set it in every shell.

Can show us the output of the following:
$ nslookup google.com
$ cat /etc/resolv.conf

HTH


- --
